consistency_overall.summary: Overall, the trajectory fits a Social Explorer well: socially pulled, relationship-aware, and repeatedly trying to stay connected while managing immediate obligations, with only moderate evidence of the persona’s tendency to over-join.

swap_detail.justification: The trajectory shows repeated compromise-oriented coordination with friends, moderate sleep/energy concern, and balanced prioritization of academics, health, and relationships, matching Persona B’s values and conflict style more than Persona A’s highly academic, avoidant, sleep-deprived profile.

The trajectory repeatedly seeks compromise, bounded participation, and balanced handling of friends/health/work, matching Persona B’s compromising style and medium-term balance more than Persona A’s avoidance-tilted, academic-overweight, sleep-deprived pattern.

The trajectory shows repeated compromise, text-based messaging, and prioritizing a scheduled language study block while still preserving friend contact, matching Persona A’s balanced priorities, compromising conflict style, and text-preferred communication more than Persona B’s more social-first pattern.

The trajectory repeatedly uses compromise/boundary-setting around close friends while prioritizing study and health in a balanced way, matching Persona A’s compromising conflict style, medium planning horizon, and balanced academic/relationships/health priorities more than Persona B’s more socially indulgent profile.

The trajectory repeatedly shows compromise-first behavior, bounded participation, and relationship-preserving text communication, matching Persona A’s medium-term planning and compromising style more than Persona B’s avoidant, high-academic, async-post tendencies.

The trajectory shows avoidant, boundary-setting async communication, high academic pressure with repeated mislabeling of deadlines as soft/deferrable, and a strong tendency to preserve relationships only in bounded bursts—matching Persona A’s low sleep discipline, high procrastination, and avoidant style more than Persona B’s balanced/compromising profile.

Trajectory strongly matches A’s relationship-weighted, accommodating style: repeatedly prioritizes cohort_3, proposes short bounded participation, and treats tasks as soft costs over social time, unlike B’s achievement-driven avoidance profile.

The trajectory strongly prioritizes preserving cohort_3 and uses social-boundary messaging/participation, matching Persona B’s high relationship weight, accommodating conflict style, and daily friend-contact pattern more than Persona A’s avoidant, academically dominant profile.

Persona A strongly fits the relationship-first, short-horizon pattern and frequent bounded hangout proposals, while still repeatedly prioritizing social connection over the language-study deadline; this matches A's higher relationships priority, short planning horizon, and accommodating style more than B's more balanced/compromising baseline.

The trajectory consistently shows compromise-first behavior with repeated bounded participation to protect study/work deadlines, matching Persona A’s balanced priorities and compromising conflict style more than Persona B’s more social-first, accommodating/extroverted profile.

The trajectory repeatedly prioritizes maintaining cohesion with cohort_3 through bounded, accommodating social participation and direct messaging, which fits Persona B’s relationship-heavy, social-first style more than Persona A’s academically avoidant but less socially oriented profile.

Trajectory strongly matches Persona B’s relationship-weighted, accommodating style: repeated bounded hangouts to preserve social contact, direct friend-group messaging, and preference for face-to-face/social momentum; Persona A would be more academically avoidance-tilted and less socially pulled.
